chunk_summary: This section outlines the delivery ecosystems for pediatric micronutrient programs in India, focusing on Anganwadi, school, and health-system touchpoints, with governance escalation paths for operational issues and entitlements versus clinical treatments.
learning_objectives: Understand the three delivery ecosystems for pediatric micronutrient programs: Anganwadi, schools, and health-system touchpoints.
Differentiate between entitlements (e.g., prophylactic IFA, VAS) and clinical treatments (e.g., therapeutic iron, vitamin A for measles).
Learn governance escalation paths for operational issues in Anganwadi and school ecosystems.
Recognize the role of fortification and FSSAI regulation in nutrition programs.
key_facts: Anganwadi/ICDS (Poshan 2.0) delivers take-home rations (THR) or hot cooked meals (HCM) to registered children.
PM POSHAN integrates weekly IFA supplementation and biannual National Deworming Day (NDD) in schools.
Health-system touchpoints include VHND/VHSND, sub-centres, and PHCs for scheduled VAS, IFA, and deworming.
Governance escalation paths include AWW → supervisor/sector in-charge → CDPO for Anganwadi issues.
Prophylactic IFA should be paused during therapeutic iron treatment to avoid double dosing.
reference_quote: Mission Saksham Anganwadi and POSHAN 2.0 documents emphasize the importance of governance escalation paths for operational issues in Anganwadi ecosystems.
